use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use airgeddon because multi-use bash script for Linux systems to audit wireless networks.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use airgeddon as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `airgeddon [options] [args]` as documented upstream for airgeddon.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair airgeddon with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `airgeddon --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
